title: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit High
system: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit High
possible_causes: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit High circuit or operation fault.
Wiring, connector, power/ground, related sensor, solenoid, actuator, or switch fault.
ATF level/condition, hydraulic system, mechanical component, TCM/PCM, or related DTC influence.
check_order: Save DTCs, freeze-frame data, ATF temperature, range state, pressure command/actual data, and related DTCs.
Confirm vehicle-specific terminals, reference values, measurement conditions, range sensor alignment, and solenoid locations in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect power/ground, signal circuits, wiring, connectors, ATF level/condition, and commanded operation.
Separate circuit, range sensor, pressure solenoid, hydraulic, and mechanical causes using the manufacturer procedure.
common_mistakes: Treating 4WD range naming or transfer case state as vehicle-universal.
Skipping actuator, switch, and mechanical range checks.
Checking only electrical circuits without separating transfer case causes.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot transmission components, high hydraulic pressure, rotating parts, and 4WD driveline components; secure the vehicle, allow cooling, relieve pressure, and follow the manufacturer procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance

Confirm vehicle-specific terminals, reference values, measurement conditions, range sensor alignment, and solenoid locations in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect power/ground, signal circuits, wiring, connectors, ATF level/condition, and commanded operation.
Separate circuit, range sensor, pressure solenoid, hydraulic, and mechanical causes using the manufacturer procedure.
common_mistakes: Treating solenoid letters or pressure circuits as vehicle-universal.
Skipping ATF temperature, pressure measurement, or enable-condition checks.
Checking only electrical circuits without separating hydraulic causes.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot transmission components, high hydraulic pressure, rotating parts, and 4WD driveline components; secure the vehicle, allow cooling, relieve pressure, and follow the manufacturer procedure.
